title: /_ping handler: return OSType http header
body:
The docker client expects to read the OSType header from the `/_ping` response in order to determine the OS type of the server, for example, when running `docker run --device=/dev/fuse ...`

https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/client/ping.go#L57

This addresses https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19767

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
> @chnrxn Thanks for the PR, could you please add a test for this as well, a sample test should be somewhere here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/apiv2/01-basic.at, let me know if you need any help :)


Hi @flouthoc , does this look ok? I thought of adding the test after the `Method not allowed` section.

```
# Method not allowed
t POST   /_ping                    405
t DELETE /_ping                    405
t POST   libpod/containers/json    405
t POST   libpod/pods/abc           405
t POST   info                      405
t GET    libpod/containers/create  405

# _ping - look for this line in the headers
#   Ostype: linux
t GET ping 200
if grep -qi ^ostype $WORKDIR/curl.headers.out; then
    _show_ok 1 "HTTP-header-OSType=present"
else
    _show_ok 0 "HTTP-header-OSType" "present" "missing"
fi
```

If you are OK, I'll amend the PR.

Hi @chnrxn , Thanks for looking at this, yes patch LGTM and I can't think of an easier way. Please amend it.

However be prepared to change it when someone other than me takes a looks and suggests a better way. @edsantiago Might be able to suggest a better way.
@chnrxn On next push if someone requests a change. Please also merge two commits into one. Other than that PR LGTM
